reviews.review_title: Now i understand the very low price
reviews.review_description: The game feels like an old-school exploration shooter for PC/console systems, and that's only a virtue. The graphics aren't groundbreaking, but they're not bad either; they're decent, and since graphics aren't the most important aspect of a video game, that's acceptable. However, it does have significant drawbacks, such as: movements are too slow and there is no option to increase speed. The enemy movements are poorly executed, lacking animation when hit by your bullets, and the patterns are quite predictable, making combat rather boring. You can find solace in exploration, which seems (for now) to be the best part of the entire game.

reviews.review_title: Cool idea, under cooked, but fair price
reviews.review_description:
feels like an alpha version made by new dev's. but the price reflects that. It would have been nice if they updated this and didn't seemingly(?) abandon it so quickly. The sound effects are very rough, guns sound are weak, your suit makes this very annoying mechanical hiss sound anytime you move, and all the voice acting is very rough AI text to speech. That said, this is the absolute closest thing on the app store to a 40k Space Hulk vr, and that alone is pretty rad. I think if you have some friends to play with, the multiplayer might be more fun than the campaign, but I don't know, I didn't test it. Also the campaign gets pretty difficult, those aliens are quick. 

Pros:
Space Hulk VR.
Co-op customization.

Cons:
Under baked, little to no polish.
Very rough sound design.

I wish I could do 3.5 stars.

It's a interesting game if you like space semi horror / action. 

To developers,
Suggest if you can lower the muzzle flash option. The current muzzle flash it's too bright and big. Hard to aim.

And one important question, how many players can play up to in co-op mission? It didn't specify.
Ive only played a few missions so far but its been pretty awesome and im looking forward to finishing it.

This was one of my favorite Quest games.   The main reason I bought it, in the first place, was because it worked offline.  I only buy offline titles because I don't always have internet for my Quest and expect the titles I purchase to work anytime and place.   "Nova Sol",  just automatically updated to 2.0 and now suddenly requires the internet to load and play my SINGLE PLAYER CAMPAIGN. 
What's really dumb is after the game loads you can disconnect the internet connection and it still works.  You can even exit/close the app.  and reload it from the quick launcher, (as long as the icon is there),  offline and it works.  But when you go to your app.  library the large icon is grayed out until an internet connection is made.  There is no reason why the SINGLE PLAYER CAMPAIGN needs the internet, in fact it doesn't.  I purchased this as an offline game and I didn't willingly update this to 2.0.
It just updated on its own.  
Obviously,  the online multi-player is nice and the faster walking ability quite good too !  But, I purchased an offline SINGLE Player Campaign  title and would really like that option back !!  That being said this is really an absolutely fantastic shooter and I've had lots of terrifying hours playing in hard mode....

developer_response.body: Thank you for your review. We will check, if we can remove the requirement of an internet connection with the next update. Should be possible.

reviews.review_description:
Graphics 🌟🌟🌟🌟🌟

For a person that’s into “lost in space” movies and an interest in space station alien shooter combat. THIS GAME IS FOR YOU!!!! 

Price: 🌟🌟🌟🌟 

In the future: Maybe Co-Op story mode gameplay to save from repetitive walk and shoot mode. Other than that. Keep going!! Can’t wait to see more patches.

However; sometimes when walking into the next load dock. You can get stuck before you can enter.

Thank you for the very prompt response and the patch! Although I would still prefer to have an option, and would like to go even faster, it now fits the game quite well and is much better! And what can I say - I just wanted to have a quick look, but it totally captivated me, and I played it through. The game isn't very long, but it's fun - the story and missions are varied. The aliens look great graphically. The weapon is okay, a bit simple visually, but the shooting is good. However, what I really like is the atmosphere and honestly, there are some really great epic scenes and moments in this game (e.g., the view of the last planet with all the spaceships in front of it or the mother alien)!

Great job and all in all, at $4.99, a very fair price for a good game. I had my fun, and anyone looking for an alien action shooter should definitely give it a try.

An incredibly atmospheric game that thrives mainly on perfectly tuned audio. Music and voice acting are excellent. And I really mean: excellent!
Visually, the futuristic look is very appealing to me and I liked all the different enemy types.
My only criticism is that the movement is far too slow. If the game ran at twice the speed, it would still be exciting, but you would feel more agile.
It's also a shame that the enemies you hit can't stay put or be completely torn apart and then stick to the walls everywhere. It would also be great if you could interact more with the environment: in the Harrier you could hold on to the bars or you could pick up the small containers and throw them around, knock over barrels, etc.
I also hope for more content. At 4.99 I didn't expect a long game, but I would happily pay four times as much for four times the length.
Due to the indescribable atmosphere, great SinglePlayer experience with a story and perfect audio, the title absolutely deserves five stars.

Edit: I wanted to play through the game again today, but unfortunately there is a problem: during the first wave attack after the elevator, when Violet is at the terminal and you have to hold the position together with Wildcat, I run out of ammunition and am overrun by the opponents. Is this a bug? My ammo refilled on my first playthrough.

Edit2: Thanks for the answer.
I can see the magazine and grenade perfectly. The problem occurs when the weapon's counter goes to 0 and I then change a magazine and it remains at 0. I've tried it for the seventh time now.

Edit3: Wow! Thank you very much, everything is working again now. Thanks for the quick help and thanks for this great, atmospheric, immersive single-player experience. I really hope for more.
